epigraph.quote: Then there was a long, peaceful silence + we walked out at 7.

per_stop.quote: 6.15 We've reached Stalisfield + changed supper booking from 8.15 to 7.30. Sunshine.
Horrid crossing. Delayed only one hour in the end. The French being extra thorough.
a nice quiet campsite with 2 dogs, ducks + chickens. Showers in the day. A bit of sun in the evening.
We had a good lunch at a restaurant by a river in Gray which Simon had visited twice before with Charles.
Pesmes a very pretty town with winding streets + the remains of a Roman layout at the top.
They piped salty water from the original site to the new factory, 13k as crow flies but 33 k of 2 underground wood pipes
I had a good shower.
SUNSHINE. Hooray! Our van is in shade in the morning, so it's a bit chilly. But we now need the shade in the afternoon.
We bought cakes + cherries. Lots of cherry trees with fruit.
1/4 Grand Canyon but small + green + practically no houses. Caves, water patterns in rock.
by the 1940s the town was nearly abandoned. Then artists came. It's now a beautiful town
The French even have a St. Trousers. St Pantaleon.
The campsite served coq au vin. A big party came in + sat at a long table. A flock of sheep went past.
It's very attractive + has at least 30 drinking fountains.
Blisteringly hot. Washing dried fast.
In one glance I saw 3 different types of butterflies.
a lovely Mediaeval Village. V. Good wine shop Le Cellier run by Hervé Sauvage.
Then we came down down down the hills to Plan de la Tour.
soon joined a ghastly queue going into St. Tropez.
a Mediterranean garden which has collected plants from hot places all over the world.
The boars trot along with their tails straight up in the air.
Simon took great care to position the van with one wheel on the pavement slanting past a tree.
We spent ages at the Theatre. It could hold 9000 people
we went into the cool church + wow, there was a music group there.
We moved out with me still lying in bed to a nice open, sunny view of the lake.
the campsite is by the river, so I did yoga + then we swam. We read.
We had a good supper by the Somme. Pork stew + apple pud.
A glorious last day in France. The wind was gentle, so we cycled to + from le Crotoy.
We drove to the Channel Tunnel + followed a handsome Ferrari onto the train.
Conrad had spent 2 hours playing football in 30° & gave me a very sweaty hug when I arrived.
